title: SpaceX IPO Draws More Than $100 Billion in Retail Orders
summary: Retail investors bidding for shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering have submitted more than $100 billion in orders, according to people familiar with the matter, as the potentially record-breaking debut enters the home stretch.

title: Novo’s Wegovy Pill Wins Approval in UK for Obesity Treatment
summary: Novo Nordisk A/S’s Wegovy pill was approved in the UK, opening up the obesity market to people who are wary of taking a shot.

title: Report: Pentagon False Alarm, Trump Vows to Strike Iran Again
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 4:47

title: Texas Stands to Gain If SpaceX (and Its Founder) Flourish After Historic IPO
summary: Elon Musk’s adopted home state has a lot riding on his success, too

title: BlackRock’s SpaceX IPO Share Order Said to Target $5 Billion
summary: BlackRock Inc. is seeking to buy shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering and is targeting an amount of about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, as investors jockey for allocations in what’s expected to be the biggest listing ever.

title: Now Starmer’s Rivals Can Turn Defense Into Attack
summary: The departure of one of Labour’s most loyal servants threatens to accelerate a change of prime minister

title: Who’s Really Paying for This Year’s World Cup?
summary: Want to see the World Cup final? That’ll be $10,000. Bloomberg’s Big Take host David Gura and Vanessa Perdomo get into dynamic pricing and surging costs for host countries as the games kick off. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ecuador to Cut Beer Prices 20% in World Cup Show of ‘Support’
summary: Ecuador is cutting beer prices by more than 20% through a temporary tax cut to rally “extra support” for the national football team during the World Cup, President Daniel Noboa said.

title: EM Currencies, Stock ETFs Rally as Trump Signals Deal Is Near
summary: Emerging-market assets got a boost after President Donald Trump canceled planned attacks on Iran, fueling bets that a deal is imminent.

title: Bank Stocks Hit Record Highs on US-Iran Deal Hopes, IPO Optimism
summary: Shares of US lenders reached their highest levels ever on Thursday, as investors turned optimistic about a potential deal to stop the fighting in Iran and SpaceX’s record IPO.

title: Stocks Rally as Trump Signals US-Iran Deal Is Near: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street powered a rally in stocks as oil sank after President Donald Trump signaled the US is close to a deal with Iran, fueling hopes for an end to the war that has roiled global markets.

title: SpaceX IPO Raises $75 Billion in Biggest Debut of All Time
summary: Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow and Bailey Lipschultz break down the final developments from SpaceX as the company prepares to go public with a record-breaking IPO on Friday. Elon Musk's aerospace company raised $75 billion in the IPO, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each, according to a statement on its website Thursday. When asked about what's driving the interest despite the lack of current profits, Ludlow says that many investors believe that profitable opportunities that SpaceX have set out for the future, including data centers in space, may occur even sooner that the company is predicting.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dollar Drops the Most in a Month as Trump Says the War Is Ending
summary: The dollar fell by the most in more than a month as President Donald Trump’s vow of progress on a deal to end the war with Iran eased demand for the haven.

title: US Stocks Soar as Trump Signals US-Iran Peace Deal Approaching
summary: US stocks soared to their best day since April, halting a two-day slide, as President Donald Trump said he’s called off planned military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the three-month war.

title: Real Yield 6/11/2026
summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: BNP Head of US Rates Strategy Guneet Dhingra, Parametric SMA Fixed Income Portfolio Manager Nisha Patel, JPMorgan Asset Management CIO of US GFICC Kay Herr, and Bank of America Head of Global FICC Micro Matthew McQueen. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Not On List of Countries Trump Says Have Agreed to Deal
summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Jamrisko said that while President Trump said that a deal to end conflict with Iran has been approved by a host of MidEast countries, Iran itself is not included on that list. Jamrisko says that this may be another instance of the push and pull between Iran and the US as the two sides negotiate and that any announcement from either side should be viewed with caution.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Tumbles as Trump Signals US Drawing Closer to Deal With Iran
summary: Oil plunged to the lowest since April as US President Donald Trump said that a peace deal with Iran could be signed as soon as the weekend, fueling bets for an end to the war that’s disrupted global energy shipments.

title: Victoria’s Secret Board Wins Shareholders Approval
summary: Victoria’s Secret & Co. investors have reelected all nine board members, including its board chair who faced a proxy contest, the company said Thursday.

title: Trump Says Deal With Iran Could Be Signed Within Days
summary: President Donald Trump says a deal to end the war with Iran could be signed within days. “We just made a great settlement of the war with Iran, and we’re going to be subject to finalization of documents, we should get done over the next few days,” Trump said. Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld report.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Raises $75 Billion in Biggest IPO of All Time
summary: SpaceX has made history with the biggest-ever IPO, selling 555.6 million shares at $135 each, and has a market value of $1.77 trillion. Trading is set to begin on the Nasdaq Friday. Elon Musk could now become the world's first trillionaire. Ed Ludlow has all the breaking details.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citigroup Raises $6.25 Billion in Year’s First High-Grade Bonds
summary: Citigroup Inc.’s banking unit sold $6.25 billion of bonds in its first investment-grade offering of 2026.

title: US Bonds Surge as Trump’s Vow That War Is Ending Sends Oil Lower
summary: Treasuries rallied after comments by US President Donald Trump on the war against Iran sparked a slide in oil prices that caused traders to price in lower chances of a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike this year.

title: Blackstone Said to Be in Talks to Buy Canada Property Firm H&R REIT
summary: Blackstone Inc. is in discussions with Canada’s H&R Real Estate Investment Trust about a potential acquisition, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Taking Kharg Island 'Very Doable' For US Forces in MidEast, Says Rebecca Grant
summary: Rebecca Grant, senior fellow and vice president for defense programs at the Lexington Institute, said that the US is better positioned to take over Kharg Island than it was at the start of the war and that it is 'very doable' for the forces central command has in the region. Grant said that because the US has air dominance in the region and controls all of the ways of approach, there would not need to be a big American troop presence on the island itself.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg Tech 2026 Opening Video
summary: Bloomberg Tech 2026 took place in San Francisco bringing together the leading CEOs, investors and innovators who are harnessing technology to change the world around us. The summit opened with a look at the state of the industry and AI.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SEC Proposes Eliminating Equities Trade-Through Rule
summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission proposed eliminating a rule that aims to ensure investors get better prices for their transactions.

title: New Zealand Two-Speed Growth Gets Boost From Farms as Cities Lag
summary: In 30 years working in New Zealand’s agriculture sector, Mark Leslie can’t recall a sweeter spot of strong commodity prices and cooperative weather.

title: Trump Cancels Planned Iran Strikes in Latest Reversal
summary: Jonathan Panikoff, director of the Scowcroft Middle East Security Initiative at the Atlantic Council, said that there is a chance that President Trump was pushing for something that Iranian negotiators had not 'fully signed off on' when he announced the cancellation of planned strikes on Iran. Panikoff said that despite the quickly changing narrative over a few hours, it is more likely if a deal comes together it is because of ongoing negotiations over multiple weeks.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Danantara Prices Debut Dollar Bond Despite Indonesia Market Rout
summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara has priced its debut global dollar bond, in a test of market appetite for the country’s assets following months of unease over economic policy and governance concerns.

title: Muni Market Operating Efficiently: BofA's McQueen
summary: Matthew McQueen, head of global FICC micro overseeing mortgages & securitized products, municipal banking & markets, and global credit at Bank of America, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: House Rejects Spy Powers Extension Amid Pulte Concerns
summary: Representative Greg Landsman, an Ohio Democrat, said the lapse of a law permitting foreign surveillance will make it harder for law enforcement to keep the country safe. Landsman said that during the world cup and ongoing conflict with Iran, concerns about US safety are 'very serious' and said that someone in the intelligence community or congress should stand up and 'be the adult in the room.'  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Financial Engineering Is Resembling Pre-Crisis Era, Pimco Warns
summary: The rapid expansion of complex credit structures is reminiscent of the buildup ahead of the global financial crisis, Pacific Investment Management Co.’s Dan Ivascyn warned.

title: New York Penn Station Rehab Eyes Billions in Federal Funding
summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Kaske joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The entities transforming New York’s Penn Station are slated to learn how much federal money will be directed to the project in about six months, according to Peter Cipriano, CEO of the Halmar-Skanska consortium, the renovation’s master developer. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: High-Yield Issuance Concentrated: JPMorgan's Herr
summary: Kay Herr, CIO of US GFICC at JPMorgan Asset Management, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Fears of a stagflation shock from the Middle East conflict are increasingly souring investor sentiment toward the weakest global corporate borrowers, many of which binged on cheap debt during the era of ultra-low interest rates. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Warsh Will Try and Make Both Sides Happy: Patel
summary: Nisha Patel, SMA fixed income portfolio manager at Parametric, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." (Source: Bloomberg)
